is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study is about Plasmodium falciparum blood-stage recombinant proteins as vaccine candidates.

reports_target_markers.evidence: This is a vaccine immunization study; no drug-resistance codon positions (Pfcrt, Pfdhfr, Pfdhps, Pfk13, Pfmdr1, etc.) are reported.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: No drug-resistance marker frequencies reported; only antibody titers and parasitemia measurements.

sub_country_location.evidence: No field-sample geographic frequency data; isolates are lab-adapted strains (FUP Palo Alto/Uganda, FCB-1/Colombia, FVO/Vietnam).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: No collection windows for resistance data; not applicable.

african_field_samples.evidence: Experiments used Aotus monkeys of Colombian origin and lab-adapted parasite isolates; no African human field/clinical isolates.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary experimental vaccine study in Aotus monkeys.

duplicate_risk.evidence: No indication of shared surveillance dataset.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary drug-resistance data implied; the study concerns vaccine immunization, not resistance markers.

assessment.summary: This is a 1990 vaccine immunization study testing recombinant P. falciparum blood-stage proteins (rp41 aldolase and 190N) in Aotus monkeys of Colombian origin, challenged with lab-adapted parasite isolates. It reports antibody titers and parasitemia, not drug-resistance marker frequencies, and involves no African field samples.
exclusion_reasons: No African human field/clinical isolates (uses Aotus monkeys and lab strains)
